Isa.41.10: God promises His personal presence ('I am with thee'), strength, help, and an upholding by His righteous right hand — the antidote to fear is not the absence of trouble but the presence of God.
Rom.8.29: Those whom God foreknew He predestined to be conformed to the image of His Son — Christlikeness is the goal of election.
Isa.44.3: Just as God pours water on dry ground, He pours His Spirit on those who thirst spiritually — His blessing flows to the parched and dependent.
Rom.12.2: Transformation comes through the renewing of the mind — replacing worldly thinking with God's truth so we can discern His will.
John.3.16: God's love cost Him His only Son and offers everlasting life to all who believe — substitutionary love at infinite cost, freely received by faith.
Phil.4.13: Christ Himself is the source — Paul's strength comes from union with Christ, not self-effort, and is contextually about contentment in any circumstance.
Jer.29.11: God's plans for His people are for peace and a hopeful future, even in exile — His sovereignty includes their flourishing.
Prov.3.5: Trusting with the whole heart means refusing to lean on our own understanding — surrendering autonomous reasoning to God's wisdom.
Matt.6.33: Seek God's kingdom and righteousness as the priority, and material needs follow — God provides for those who put Him first.
Rom.8.28: God orchestrates every event — even suffering — for the ultimate good of those who love Him, defined as conformity to Christ (v.29).
Phil.4.6: Anxiety is replaced by prayer with thanksgiving — believers exchange worry for petition, trusting God hears.
John.14.6: Jesus alone — He is THE way, truth, and life, the exclusive mediator between God and man.
2Cor.5.17: Anyone in Christ becomes a new creation — old identity passes away, all things become new in regeneration.
Prov.4.23: The heart is the wellspring of life; what we let into it shapes everything that flows out — guard it as the most strategic battlefield.
Gen.1.1: God — the eternal triune Creator — is the origin of all matter, time, and space; creation has a beginning and a Creator.
2Cor.6.16: Believers collectively are God's temple — He dwells in them by His Spirit, fulfilling 'I will be their God.'
Josh.23.10: Because the Lord their God fights for them — divine empowerment, not human strength, multiplies the believer's effectiveness.
Isa.40.31: Those who wait on the Lord renew their strength, mount up like eagles, run without weariness, walk without fainting — divine strength replaces human weakness.
John.1.1: The Word was eternally with God and was God — affirming Christ's eternality, distinct personhood, and full deity.
Rom.8.31: No one can stand against us — if the omnipotent God is for His people, every adversary is overwhelmed.
Luke.11.9: Persistent prayer is rewarded — God responds to those who continue asking, seeking, and knocking.
2Cor.12.9: God's strength is perfected in weakness — Paul learns to glory in his thorn because Christ's power rests on him.
Matt.7.7: Asking, seeking, knocking yields receiving, finding, opening — Jesus guarantees God's responsiveness to faithful prayer.
1Sam.15.22: Obedience is better than sacrifice — God values heart-submission over religious performance.
Isa.43.25: For His own sake, God blots out transgressions and remembers them no more — forgiveness is grounded in God's character, not the sinner's worth.
Isa.61.1: The Spirit-anointed Servant preaches good news to the poor, heals the brokenhearted, proclaims liberty — Jesus claims this in Luke 4.
Heb.8.10: In the new covenant, God writes His law on the heart and mind — internal transformation replaces external code.
Heb.13.6: With the Lord as helper, the believer fears nothing man can do — divine presence dispels human fear.
Gal.6.9: In due season we shall reap if we faint not — perseverance in well-doing eventually yields a harvest.
1Cor.16.13: Stand fast in the faith, be courageous, be strong — vigilant, manly, firm, dependent on Christ.
Matt.24.35: Christ's words outlast heaven and earth — His revelation is more permanent than the cosmos.
Gal.5.22: Love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance — the Spirit's character produced in the believer.
Ps.23.1: The Lord shepherds His people, providing every need — consequently David shall not want, for his Shepherd-King supplies fully.
1Cor.15.58: Resurrection guarantees that gospel labor is never wasted — the future hope makes present effort meaningful.
Jer.33.3: God promises to answer and show great and unsearchable things to those who call on Him — prayer accesses divine revelation.
Phil.1.6: Christ Himself completes the good work begun in believers — perseverance is divinely guaranteed, not self-sustained.
Eph.2.10: Believers were created in Christ for good works which God prepared beforehand — works are the fruit, not the root, of salvation.
Ps.1.1: The man who avoids the counsel of the wicked, the path of sinners, and the seat of mockers — blessing comes through separation from sin.
1Pet.2.9: Believers are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, God's possession — to proclaim His excellencies.
Eph.6.10: Be strong in the Lord and in the power of His might — strength is found in Christ, not self.
1Chr.28.9: God searches all hearts and understands every imagination of thoughts — He requires perfect heart and willing mind.
Josh.1.9: Because the Lord is with His people wherever they go — divine presence is the basis of courage.
2Tim.1.7: Not a spirit of fear but of power, love, and a sound mind — God's gift opposes timidity.
Isa.45.17: An everlasting salvation — Israel shall not be ashamed or confounded forever.
John.15.7: If we abide in Christ and His words abide in us, we may ask what we will and it shall be done — abiding aligns prayer with God's will.
1Pet.5.7: Because He cares for us — God's care, not His distance, is why we cast our anxiety upon Him.
Isa.55.6: While He may be found, while He is near — there is an urgency window for repentance.
Ps.27.1: The Lord is light, salvation, strength of life — those whose God is the Lord need fear no one.
Eph.3.20: Exceedingly abundantly above all we ask or think, according to the power working in us — God's capacity outstrips our imagination.
Isa.59.21: God promises His Spirit and His words shall not depart from His people, nor their seed, forever — covenant continuity through Spirit and Word.
Jer.30.18: God will rebuild and restore, and Jerusalem shall be built upon her own heap — the city restored to her ancient place.
1Pet.5.10: After a little suffering, the God of all grace makes us perfect, established, strengthened, settled — suffering is the path to maturity.
John.16.33: In Christ we have peace because He has overcome the world — His victory is the source of peace amid tribulation.
Isa.9.6: Wonderful Counsellor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace — the Messianic Child is fully divine.
John.14.15: By keeping His commandments — love and obedience are inseparable in Christ's economy.
Jas.1.12: The crown of life — the man who endures temptation and is approved receives the eternal reward promised to lovers of God.
Gal.2.20: Crucified with Christ, yet living — Christ now lives in Paul, who lives by faith in the Son of God.
Rom.12.1: Presenting our bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable — total bodily consecration is reasonable worship in light of mercies.
John.14.27: Not the world's peace but Christ's own peace — peace rooted in His person, not circumstances.
Josh.1.8: Constant meditation on the law leads to careful obedience, which yields prosperity and good success.
Isa.26.3: The one whose mind is stayed on God, because he trusts in Him — peace is the fruit of fixed trust.
Ps.51.10: A clean heart and a right spirit — David asks for re-creation, not just forgiveness.
Ps.91.1: The one who dwells in the secret place of the Most High — abiding under His shadow is intentional, sustained intimacy.
Jer.15.16: Joy and rejoicing of heart — internalized Word produces deep delight, especially in suffering.
Mark.16.16: Salvation — believing and being baptized go together as the public profession of inward faith.
1Pet.5.8: Sober vigilance — the devil prowls like a roaring lion seeking to devour, demanding alertness.
Heb.4.12: Pierces dividing soul, spirit, joints, marrow — discerns thoughts and intents of the heart.
Eph.1.4: Before the foundation of the world — God's election precedes creation itself.
John.1.14: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us — His glory full of grace and truth made the unseen God known.
2Tim.2.19: The Lord knows them that are His, and let everyone naming His name depart from iniquity — election and ethics, double-sealed.
Matt.9.13: Sinners — Jesus came not for the self-righteous but for those who recognize their need.
2Chr.7.14: He will hear, forgive sin, and heal the land — the conditions are humility, prayer, seeking His face, and turning from wickedness.
Ps.84.11: No good thing — God supplies sun, shield, grace, glory; nothing genuinely good is withheld from those who walk uprightly.
Mic.6.8: To do justice, love mercy, walk humbly with God — the threefold summary of true religion.
John.8.12: Whoever follows Christ shall not walk in darkness but have the light of life.
Ps.33.10: The Lord brings to nought the counsel of nations and frustrates the devices of peoples — sovereign rule over geopolitics.
Ps.55.22: He sustains them and never permits the righteous to be moved — divine support replaces self-effort.
Heb.13.5: Because God says 'I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee' — His abiding presence makes covetousness senseless.
Ps.37.4: The desires of the heart — when delight is in the Lord, our desires conform to His will and He fulfills them.
Phil.4.19: According to His riches in glory by Christ Jesus — supply matches divine riches, not human shortfall.
Jer.1.5: He formed us in the womb, knew us before we existed, ordained us — divine knowledge precedes biological existence.
Heb.12.1: With endurance, laying aside every weight and the besetting sin, looking unto Jesus the author and finisher.
Joel.2.28: All flesh — sons, daughters, old and young, servants — fulfilled at Pentecost (Acts 2).
Isa.48.17: By His commands and the way He leads — divine pedagogy produces practical profit.
Isa.44.18: They have not known nor understood; their eyes are shut and their hearts are darkened — judicial blindness from idolatry.
Matt.11.28: All who labor and are heavy laden — the universally invited weary find rest in Him.
Heb.11.6: Without faith it is impossible to please God — faith is the prerequisite of relationship.
1John.5.14: Asking according to His will — alignment with God's purpose is the condition of confident prayer.
Rom.5.8: While we were yet sinners, Christ died for us — God's love is demonstrated apart from any worthiness in us.
Luke.4.18: Preach the gospel to the poor, heal the brokenhearted, deliver captives, restore sight, free the oppressed — Messianic mission unveiled.
Zech.13.9: Through fire — He brings the third part through, refining as silver, then they call on His name and He answers.
Prov.29.23: Pride brings a man low; honor upholds the humble — divine reversal is the moral order.
1Cor.10.13: A way of escape — God limits temptation to what we can bear and provides exit.
Jer.31.33: Inwardly — God puts His law in their inward parts and writes it on their hearts in the new covenant.
Heb.12.2: Jesus, the author and finisher of faith, who endured the cross for the joy set before Him.
Deut.4.9: Lest we forget what our eyes have seen — generational memory is a guard against apostasy.
John.10.28: Eternal life and security — none shall pluck them out of His hand.
Eph.2.8: By grace through faith — and even faith itself is the gift of God, not of works.
Jas.5.16: The effectual fervent prayer of a righteous man — confessed sin and earnest intercession unleash power.
John.15.16: Christ chose us — election precedes our response, and bears fruit that remains.
Col.3.16: Richly — in all wisdom, teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs.
Phil.2.13: God Himself — He works in us both the willing and the doing of His good pleasure.
John.11.25: Jesus — He IS the resurrection and the life; whoever believes in Him, though dead, shall live.
Ps.31.19: Great enough that it is laid up — God stores up goodness for those who fear Him, manifest before men.
Jas.1.17: From above, from the Father of lights, with whom is no variation or shadow of turning — God's giving is unchanging.
2Tim.1.9: Not according to our works but according to His own purpose and grace given in Christ before the world began.
1Cor.2.9: Things eye has not seen, ear has not heard, nor has it entered the heart of man — beyond imagination.
Deut.31.6: He will not fail us nor forsake us — His covenant fidelity grounds our courage.
2Tim.3.16: Doctrine, reproof, correction, instruction in righteousness — Scripture is God-breathed and sufficient for godliness.
Ezek.36.26: A new heart of flesh, replacing the heart of stone — and a new Spirit within.
Rom.8.1: Because they walk not after the flesh but after the Spirit — Christ's substitutionary death exhausted condemnation.
Matt.5.3: The poor in spirit — those bankrupt of self-righteousness — inherit the kingdom of heaven.
Jer.32.17: Nothing is too hard for the God who made heaven and earth by His great power.
1John.2.15: Because love of the world is incompatible with love of the Father — they are mutually exclusive affections.
Ps.27.14: Strengthens the heart — waiting on the Lord builds inner courage.
2Cor.5.21: He who knew no sin became sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in Him — the Great Exchange.
1Pet.2.24: He bore our sins in His body on the tree, that we, dying to sins, might live to righteousness — by His stripes we are healed.
Heb.11.1: The substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen — faith makes the future certain in the present.
Prov.3.6: He directs our paths — divine guidance follows total acknowledgment.
Mark.8.18: Because they have eyes but see not, ears but hear not — spiritual perception is gift, not faculty.
Mark.11.24: Belief that one has received it — confident faith, not doubting prayer.
1John.4.4: Greater is He who is in you than he who is in the world — indwelling Spirit overpowers cosmic opposition.
Phil.4.8: Whatever is true, honorable, just, pure, lovely, of good report, virtuous, praiseworthy — sanctification of imagination.
Isa.43.1: Because He has redeemed us and called us by name — we are His personal possession.
Acts.1.8: Power to be Christ's witnesses in Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria, and the ends of the earth — Spirit-empowered global mission.
Phil.4.7: Peace that passes understanding — surpassing rational comprehension, guarding heart and mind in Christ Jesus.
Jas.1.5: Liberally and without reproach — God gives wisdom freely to those who ask.
Isa.54.17: None — every weapon formed against God's people fails, and every accusing tongue is condemned.
Col.3.1: On things above where Christ is at God's right hand — heavenly orientation defines the risen life.
Matt.28.19: Make disciples of all nations, baptizing in the triune name and teaching obedience to all Christ commanded.
Eph.3.16: By His Spirit through the riches of His glory — divine power in the inner man.
Ps.37.5: He brings it to pass — committing our way unleashes God's sovereign performance.
Eph.4.22: The old man corrupted by deceitful lusts — pre-conversion identity must be deliberately discarded.
Jer.29.12: He will hear them — the seeking heart finds the listening God.
Eph.6.18: Always, with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, watching with all perseverance for all saints — comprehensive intercession.
Jas.3.17: Pure, peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, without hypocrisy.
John.7.37: Anyone who thirsts — Christ's invitation is universal, but personal.
Zeph.3.19: He undoes oppressors and gathers the afflicted into praise — divine reversal of fortunes.
Acts.2.38: Repent and be baptized in Jesus' name for remission of sins — turning and public identification.
Isa.30.21: By a voice behind us saying 'This is the way, walk in it' — His Spirit guides moment by moment.
Rom.12.12: Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, continue instant in prayer — three postures for suffering.
Acts.26.18: To open eyes, turn from darkness to light, from Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness and inheritance.
Col.3.17: In word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father.
Isa.57.15: With him of contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble and the heart of the contrite.
Isa.43.2: He is with us — the waters do not overflow nor the fire kindle on us, because of His presence.
1John.4.9: By God sending His only Son into the world that we might live through Him — the incarnation embodies love.
John.14.21: Whoever has and keeps His commandments — Christ manifests Himself to obedient lovers.
Prov.16.3: Our thoughts are established — God orders the planning when works are entrusted to Him.
Ps.46.1: God Himself — a very present help in trouble, refuge and strength.
Isa.40.29: To those who have none — God supplies the faint and weak with His own strength.
Ezek.36.25: By sprinkling clean water upon them — symbolic of Spirit-given purification, fulfilled in regeneration.
2Cor.4.4: The god of this world has blinded their minds — Satan's deception keeps the gospel from being seen.
Matt.6.25: Because life is more than food and the body more than raiment — God who gave life will sustain it.
Matt.5.6: Those who hunger and thirst after righteousness — they shall be filled with God's righteousness in Christ.
Jer.17.7: The man whose hope is in the Lord — and whose trust the Lord is.
Zech.4.6: By God's Spirit — divine accomplishment, not human resource.
Isa.12.2: God is salvation, strength, song — fear is replaced by trusting confidence in Him.
Isa.55.8: Higher than ours, as the heavens are above the earth — His ways and thoughts transcend ours.
Jas.1.19: Quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath — godly speech ethics.
John.14.26: All things that Christ said — the Spirit's teaching ministry confirms and recalls Christ's words.
Jas.4.8: He draws near to us — proximity is reciprocal in covenant relationship.
Isa.60.1: Because her light has come and the glory of the Lord is risen upon her — eschatological dawn.
Exod.34.6: Merciful, gracious, longsuffering, abundant in goodness and truth — the foundational creedal description of Yahweh.
Num.23.19: He is not man that He should lie nor son of man that He should repent — divine unchangeability.
Col.3.12: Bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering — the wardrobe of the elect.
Matt.28.18: All authority in heaven and on earth — universal cosmic dominion.
Matt.11.27: The Son reveals the Father to whom He wills — knowledge of God is mediated through Christ.
John.1.12: As many as received Him, who believe on His name — receiving and believing are functionally identical.
Jer.29.13: When we seek Him with all our heart — wholehearted pursuit guarantees finding.
Eph.4.29: That which is good for edification, that it may minister grace to the hearers — speech as a means of grace.
John.10.10: Abundant life — Christ comes that His sheep may have life and have it more abundantly.
Matt.7.21: He who does the will of the Father — confession without obedience is empty.
John.15.5: Nothing — apart from abiding in Christ the Vine, branches bear no fruit.
Isa.44.2: By saying 'Fear not, Jacob My servant, and Jeshurun whom I have chosen' — election grounds comfort.
1Cor.8.3: He who loves God — being known by God is the deeper reality than knowing about God.
Ps.145.18: All who call on Him in truth — sincere prayer accesses divine nearness.
Matt.5.16: So that men see good works and glorify the Father in heaven — visible holiness aimed at God's glory.
John.3.36: The wrath of God — those who reject the Son shall not see life; God's wrath remains on them.
Exod.14.14: Israel needs only hold their peace — God Himself wages war for them.
John.10.27: They hear His voice, He knows them, they follow — recognition, knowledge, obedience.
Eccl.11.6: Because we know not which shall prosper — diversified obedience yields harvest from God.
2Cor.9.8: All grace abounds — God supplies sufficiency in all things at all times for every good work.
Titus.2.14: To redeem us from all iniquity and purify a peculiar people zealous of good works.
John.4.14: Becomes a well of water springing up to everlasting life — internal, eternal, perpetual.
Ps.23.4: Because the Shepherd is with us — His rod and staff comfort, His presence dispels fear.
Mic.7.18: None — Yahweh's pardoning grace is unique among all gods.
Luke.18.1: Persistent prayer guards against discouragement — the unjust judge parable teaches God's responsiveness.
Acts.4.12: Jesus' name alone — there is no other name under heaven by which we must be saved.
Rev.13.8: Those whose names were written in the Lamb's book of life from the foundation of the world — election preceding creation.
2Cor.3.18: By beholding His glory unveiled — we are transformed from glory to glory by the Spirit.
1John.5.20: Him that is true — and we are in Him that is true, in His Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
Lam.3.25: To them that wait for Him, to the soul that seeks Him — divine goodness meets seeking faith.
Isa.44.6: None — Yahweh alone, the King and Redeemer of Israel, the LORD of hosts.
1John.1.7: Cleanses us from all sin — fellowship in light is sustained by continual cleansing through the blood.
Luke.19.10: To seek and save the lost — the Son of Man's mission stated in one sentence.
Ps.32.8: With His eye upon us — gentle guidance contrasted with the bridle of the unwilling.
Rom.15.13: All joy and peace in believing, that we may abound in hope through the Holy Spirit's power.
Jas.1.2: All joy — because trial of faith works patience that perfects us.
Rev.1.5: By His own blood — the faithful witness, firstborn from the dead, ruler of earth's kings.
Eph.1.17: The spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Christ — illumined understanding.
Rom.8.32: All things — He who gave His Son will surely give all lesser gifts.
Ps.46.10: Be still, and know that I am God — cessation of striving as worship.
Eph.4.32: As God for Christ's sake forgave us — divine forgiveness is the model and motivation.
Isa.26.20: In secret chambers, until indignation overpasses — temporary withdrawal under judgment.
Gal.5.1: The liberty wherewith Christ has made us free — freedom from law's curse and sin's bondage.
Ps.50.15: He delivers us, and we glorify Him — distress is the occasion for divine glory.
Luke.1.37: Nothing — every word from God shall be performed (the Greek echoes Gen 18:14).
1Sam.7.3: Foreign gods and Ashtoreths, and prepare hearts to serve Him only — exclusive devotion.
John.14.13: Whatever is asked in His name He will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
Eph.6.12: Principalities, powers, rulers of darkness, spiritual wickedness in heavenly places — not flesh and blood.
Col.1.13: The kingdom of darkness — translated into the kingdom of His dear Son.
Luke.11.28: Those who hear the word of God and keep it — even more blessed than physical proximity to Jesus.
Gen.15.1: Abraham's exceeding great reward — God Himself, not just His gifts.
John.8.32: Makes them free — known truth liberates from sin's bondage.
John.1.29: The Lamb of God — fulfilling Passover and Levitical typology.
2Cor.6.14: Because righteousness has no fellowship with unrighteousness, light no communion with darkness.
Matt.21.22: Believing prayer — faith is the channel through which prayer is answered.
1Cor.6.9: The unrighteous — fornicators, idolaters, adulterers, etc.; sanctified believers are washed and inherit.
Matt.28.20: To be with them always, even to the end of the age — His presence sustains the mission.
Luke.10.19: To tread on serpents and scorpions and over all the power of the enemy — kingdom delegation.
John.7.38: Rivers of living water — fulfilled by the Spirit given to those who believed.
John.4.10: The water of eternal life — the Spirit Himself.
Eph.1.7: Christ — through His blood, redemption and forgiveness according to the riches of grace.
Titus.3.5: Not according to works of righteousness, but by the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Spirit.
1Cor.3.16: They are God's temple, and His Spirit dwells in them.
Mal.3.6: Because the Lord changes not — divine immutability preserves Israel.
Isa.55.7: Mercy and abundant pardon — let the wicked forsake his way and return to the Lord.
Heb.7.25: Uttermost — He saves completely those who come to God through Him, ever living to intercede.
Isa.43.19: He makes a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert — unprecedented redemption.
Ps.25.3: Those who wait on the Lord — divine fidelity ensures the hopeful are not disappointed.
Isa.46.10: God alone — declaring 'My counsel shall stand and I will do all My pleasure.'
Col.1.11: His glorious power — unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness.
1Pet.2.5: A holy and royal priesthood — offering spiritual sacrifices acceptable through Jesus Christ.
1Pet.1.22: Fervently, with a pure heart — purified souls love deeply through obedience to truth.
John.6.27: Not the food that perishes but that which endures unto everlasting life, which the Son of Man gives.
Eph.6.11: To stand against the wiles of the devil — armor enables defensive strength.
Eph.1.11: Him who works all things after the counsel of His own will — God's sovereign decree determines all.
John.3.3: Being born again of water and Spirit — regeneration is necessary to perceive God's reign.
Col.1.15: Christ — the firstborn of all creation, supreme over what is created.
2Cor.4.17: A far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory — present pain produces future glory beyond comparison.
John.15.4: Abiding in the Vine — branches cannot bear fruit unless connected to Christ.
Isa.53.5: By His stripes — He was wounded for our transgressions, bruised for our iniquities, chastised for our peace.
Col.3.10: In knowledge after the image of Him that created him — sanctification renews knowledge into Christ's likeness.
Prov.20.22: To save us — vengeance belongs to God, not retaliation to us.
Exod.15.11: None — Yahweh is incomparable in holiness, glory, and wonder.
Matt.19.26: All things — what is impossible with men is possible with God.
1Cor.1.30: Wisdom, righteousness, sanctification, and redemption from God — Christ is fourfold provision.
2Cor.10.4: Mighty through God to pull down strongholds — spiritual, not carnal weapons.
1Cor.13.4: Patient, kind, not envious, not boastful, not proud — love defined by virtue and absence of vice.
Luke.6.45: From the abundance of the heart — the mouth reveals heart treasure or corruption.
John.17.3: Knowing the only true God and Jesus Christ whom He sent — eternal life is relational, not merely durational.
2Cor.10.5: Every imagination — bringing every thought into obedience to Christ.
1Pet.3.18: To bring us to God — the just for the unjust, once for all in His finished work.
Heb.13.8: He is the same yesterday, today, and forever — Christ's immutability anchors hope.
Rev.5.9: Out of every kindred, tongue, people, and nation — the Lamb's redemption is global.
1Sam.16.7: The heart — God's evaluation transcends external appearance.
John.13.34: Love one another as Christ loved us — His sacrificial love is the new standard.
Jas.1.22: Doers, not merely hearers — passive hearing without doing is self-deception.
Rom.5.1: Peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ — the legal hostility ends.
1Pet.1.3: A living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead — hope grounded in Christ's risen life.
Col.3.23: Heartily, as to the Lord and not unto men — vocational worship in every task.
Gal.6.7: What is sown is reaped — God is not mocked; sowing flesh reaps corruption, sowing Spirit reaps life.
Ezek.11.19: One heart and a new spirit, replacing the heart of stone with a heart of flesh.
Matt.7.24: Whoever hears Christ's words and does them — wise foundation that withstands storms.
John.5.24: Everlasting life — passage from death to life, no condemnation.
Isa.55.11: What He pleases — His Word does not return void; it accomplishes its purpose.
Jer.17.9: Deceitful above all things and desperately wicked — only God can know it fully.
1Cor.13.13: Love — abides longer than faith and hope, which terminate in sight and possession.
Eph.2.4: Because of His great love and rich mercy — divine love precedes our deserving.
Gen.18.14: Nothing — the rhetorical question affirms God's omnipotence concerning Sarah's promise.
Deut.6.5: With all heart, soul, and might — totalistic, undivided love.
Mark.16.15: All the world, every creature — universal proclamation mandate.
Prov.18.21: Death and life — words have life-giving or destroying power.
Ps.19.7: Converts the soul — perfect, sure, restoring it.
Col.1.10: Bearing fruit in every good work, growing in the knowledge of God — fruitfulness with knowledge.
Ps.118.6: Nothing of consequence — the Lord on our side defeats human opposition.
John.10.11: Jesus — He lays down His life for the sheep, contrasted with hirelings.
Acts.2.17: His Spirit on all flesh — sons and daughters prophesy, young see visions, old dream dreams.
Rom.8.2: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus — frees from the law of sin and death.
Ps.1.3: A tree planted by rivers of water, bringing forth fruit, leaf not withering, prospering.
Prov.18.10: A strong tower — the righteous run into it and are safe.
Ps.34.4: He delivers from all fears — seeking is met with answering and deliverance.
